purpose: This commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth subsystem by preventing queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary include directive in a source file.
The commit updates documentation for store metrics, adding a new metric 'N/A'.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
This commit adds two new supply entries to the realtek,rt5682s binding file for the AVDD and MICVDD supplies.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call to release the mapped memory.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the MLX5 driver.
This commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed, which avoids a warning from static checkers.
This commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V architecture.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
The commit modifies the RTC test suite to skip tests when the RTC is not present.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to a variable called 'bytes' in the echoaudio driver.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks.
The commit removes duplicated text in a comment within the spte.h file.
The commit reverts changes to a table used for clock management in the display driver, specifically the dcn315_clk_mgr module.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit modifies several files in the Linux kernel's PCIe driver to use a new data structure for representing PCIe controllers.
The commit fixes a warning in the clang compiler by changing the format specifier for an unsigned short variable.

The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture's jump label transformation function by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long to do signed compare and avoid negative offset triggering a warning.
The commit removes a case in SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function to set a hardware register for P2P power save offload. The change is part of an ongoing effort to remove the HAL layer.
The commit fixes a bug in the arm64 architecture by declaring non-global symbols as static.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from display subsystem nodes in device tree bindings for MSM8998 and QCM2290.
The commit modifies the V4L2 controls API to allow for dynamic allocation of arrays in struct ctrl_ref.
The commit adds new PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver, which is responsible for providing information about the system-on-chip (SoC) and its components.
This commit adds support for recognizing all possible chip cuts in the Realtek RTL8xxxU driver.
The commit removes a custom software enable count from PRCMU clocks, as the core already keeps a similar count.
The commit modifies the initialization of memory management for RISC-V processors, specifically by removing unnecessary conditional code related to XIP (eXecute In Place) kernel usage.
The commit adds support for QUP and GPI DMA hardware to enable functioning I2C nodes.
The commit fixes a bug in the thermal driver by replacing a confusing comment with a more accurate description of the code's purpose.
The commit fixes an infinite loop in the release_swfw_sync function by acquiring the HW semaphore before releasing it.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology.
The commit removes a hack that was used to support future NFS swap support.
The commit implements GTP echo responses for GTPv0 and GTPv1.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_*() functions with cpumask_first_*() where appropriate. The changes are made to improve code readability and reduce the number of lines.
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which would cause a failure message to be displayed on the console.

This commit adds support for a new platform without amplifier by adding a new board config adl_rt5682 to support alc5682 headset codec without speaker amplifier. It follows Intel BT offload design by connecting alc5682 to SSP0.
This commit adds a new check to fail zerocopy send requests for protocols that don't support it.
The commit adds a test to check if xeCJK.sty exists before loading it, which is useful on RHEL 9 and its clone distros where texlive-xecjk doesn't work at the moment due to a missing dependency.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros in kernel code.
The commit replaces the simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from the codebase.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank
The commit fixes a bug in the 'hqd_destroy' function by changing the type of the 'reset_type' parameter from 'uint32_t' to 'enum kfd_preempt_type', which is the correct type for this parameter.

The commit fixes a bug in the device tree bindings for STMicroelectronics STM32 processors by correcting the name of a property from 'reset' to 'resets'. The change also adds an additionalProperties: false constraint to ensure that only valid properties are used.
The commit fixes a warning in the GCC compiler by silencing it.
The commit modifies the use of pm_runtime_get_sync() to pm_runtime_resume_and_get() in sun8i-ss-prng.c.
This commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring to its own filetable.c file.
The commit splits the VPU decoder into two separate nodes: G1 and G2. Each node has its own power-domain controller and can be independently fused out.

The commit removes a macro called IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON and replaces it with a new definition for IWL_DEVICE_BZ. The changes made in the commit are related to the configuration of the device, specifically the LED mode and the minimum buffer size.
